name: Dioxus
description: Cross-platform (web/desktop/mobile) RSX framework with a strong web/SPA and fullstack story

description.value: Cross-platform Rust framework for building web, desktop, and mobile apps from a single codebase using an RSX macro, signals, and an integrated fullstack server-function story

latest-stable-version.comment: Latest stable as of 2026-07-19; a 0.8.0-alpha.0 prerelease was published 2026-05-19.

latest-release.comment: Release date of stable 0.7.9 (2026-05-08); the newest overall publish is the 0.8.0-alpha.0 prerelease on 2026-05-19.

maintenance-status.comment: Frequent commits and releases; repository last pushed July 2026, full-time core team.

maturity.comment: Pre-1.0 (0.7.x) with breaking changes still expected before 1.0, but shipped in production by several organizations.

corporate-foundation-backing.value: Dioxus Labs (Y Combinator S23); development supported by FutureWei, Satellite.im, and the GitHub Accelerator program

reactivity-model.comment: Signals-based state management (0.5+) layered over a VirtualDom that diffs and patches; a state change marks the owning component dirty and re-renders + diffs its subtree.

component-model.comment: Components are `fn(...) -> Element` with the #[component] macro.

view-authoring.comment: Views are declared with the rsx! {} macro; the docs state only rsx!-declared markup is supported.

fine-grained-updates.comment: Default render path uses VirtualDom diffing; signals trigger a scoped component re-render + diff rather than updating exact DOM nodes directly.

async-await-in-components.comment: use_resource / use_future plus suspense allow awaiting async data loading.

global-state-solution.comment: Shared state via context (use_context/provide_context) and global signals (Signal / GlobalSignal).

compiles-to.comment: Web target compiles to WebAssembly via wasm-bindgen (dioxus-web); non-web targets are native, out of scope here.

dev-server-hmr.comment: Subsecond Rust hot-patching plus RSX/asset hot reload; falls back to rebuild + browser refresh.

minimal-wasm-bundle-size.comment: No reproducible gzipped minimal hello-world measurement under a documented build profile (opt-level, LTO, wasm-opt) available; omitted per RESEARCH guidance rather than recording an unmethodical number.

js-interop.comment: Built on wasm-bindgen; web-sys/js-sys are accessible, and document::eval runs JS snippets from Rust.

js-interop-ergonomics.comment: document::eval helper plus wasm-bindgen/web-sys make calling JS and web APIs fairly smooth, though some boilerplate remains for raw web-sys work.

client-side-rendering-spa.comment: dioxus-web provides a pure client-rendered SPA mode.

static-site-generation.comment: SSG / incremental static generation supported via the fullstack layer.

hydration.comment: Full-tree hydration of server-rendered HTML; no islands mode.

server-functions.comment: Integrated isomorphic #[server] functions callable directly from client code.

backend-integration.comment: Fullstack integrates primarily with Axum; ships its own server wrapper around it.

isomorphic-routing.comment: The same Routable enum resolves routes identically on server and client.

official-router.comment: First-party dioxus-router, enabled via the "router" feature.

nested-routes.comment: #[nest] and #[layout] attributes with Outlet render nested layouts.

typed-routes.comment: Routes are a #[derive(Routable)] enum checked at compile time; an invalid route is a compile error.

route-guards.comment: No dedicated guard attribute; redirects via #[redirect] and global guards via RouterConfig on_update / navigator.replace.

lazy-code-split-routes.comment: Automatic per-route bundle splitting via the wasm-split feature on dioxus-router.

styling-approach.comment: Standard HTML/CSS styling; asset!()-bundled stylesheets, Tailwind-friendly, and inline style attributes. No built-in component-scoped CSS.

scoped-styles.comment: No first-party component-scoped CSS; styles are authored as plain global CSS.

component-library-ecosystem.comment: First-party component primitives (shadcn/ui-inspired) plus a few community kits (e.g. dioxus-free-icons, daisyui ports); not yet several mature full UI libraries.

forms-inputs.comment: Controlled inputs via value + oninput handlers; onsubmit exposes aggregated FormData for uncontrolled-style handling. No automatic two-way binding.

svg-canvas-support.comment: SVG elements are authorable directly in rsx!; canvas via web-sys interop.

suspense-loading-ui.comment: SuspenseBoundary for declarative loading UI around async resources.

error-boundaries.comment: ErrorBoundary component catches and renders fallback UI.

documentation-quality.comment: Structured guide/book with tutorials, essentials, and reference plus API docs and many examples; some pages lag behind fast-moving releases.

api-ergonomics.comment: Idiomatic rsx! + function components and Copy signals keep boilerplate low; occasional macro/lifetime friction.

compile-times.comment: Macro-heavy but comparable to peer Rust web frameworks; hot-patching mitigates iteration cost during development.

examples-count.comment: The examples/ directory is organized into 10 numbered category folders (UI, state, async, routing, fullstack, integrations, etc.) containing well over 20 examples.

interactive-playground.comment: Official Dioxus playground (server-compiled, shareable) hosted from the docsite.

browser-devtools.comment: dioxus-devtools powers hot-reload tooling, but there is no dedicated component-tree/signal inspector browser extension beyond generic browser tools.

testing-support.comment: Components can be exercised via VirtualDom in native tests; wasm-bindgen-test works for in-browser WASM tests.

crates-io-downloads.comment: All-time downloads of the top-level dioxus crate as of 2026-07-19.

contributors.comment: Approximate GitHub contributor count (incl. anonymous) as of 2026-07-19.

ecosystem-crates.comment: Official router, fullstack, signals/stores, and CLI plus a growing set of community integrations and component kits.

notable-production-users.value: Airbus & the European Space Agency (collision-avoidance system), Huawei, and Satellite.im (Uplink)
notable-production-users.comment: Per Dioxus Labs' Y Combinator profile.

meta-framework-full-stack-kit.comment: First-party fullstack layer with server functions, SSR/SSG, and hydration.
